design_decision: Install a CDR adaptability/interface-contract gate that denies code acceptance if public/runtime interfaces lack versioned contracts, compatibility/migration policy, and change-impact evidence.
synthesis: CDR adaptability must be enforceable through explicit interfaces, compatibility policy, deprecation plans, extension points, versioning rules, and migration evidence.
Interface contracts must declare inputs, outputs, errors, side effects, compatibility guarantees, ownership, and examples so future stages can modify code safely.
Adaptability is not accepted as an adjective; it requires measurable extension and change-impact evidence.
